metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating proximal biceps tendon pathology.
Proximal biceps pathology presents with anterior shoulder pain and Popeye deformity after rupture.
Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for proximal biceps tendon pathology — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for proximal biceps tendon pathology.
Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for proximal biceps tendon pathology require attending-defined progression gates.
Distal migration of the biceps muscle belly suggests long head rupture (Popeye sign).
